aggregator.name: aggregator

hops.description: Number of swap events in the transaction. 1 for direct pool swaps, >1 for router/aggregator paths.
hops.name: hops

token_bought.description: Symbol of the token received on the last hop of the transaction.
token_bought.name: token_bought

token_sold.description: Symbol of the token paid in on the first hop of the transaction.
token_sold.name: token_sold

trade_usd.description: Conservative USD notional for the trade (max across hops of the per-hop conservative notional).
trade_usd.name: trade_usd

trader.description: Transaction origin address (`tx.from_address`) — the EOA that initiated the swap.
trader.name: trader

via.description: Comma-separated list of DEX protocols touched by the transaction.
via.name: via

api_execution_live_trades.description: Dashboard-facing feed of recent DEX trades on Gnosis Chain. Reads from the cached `int_live__dex_trades_raw` table, so user queries never hit source logs or trigger decode-log scans. Last 30 minutes of CACHED data (anchored on `max(block_timestamp)` in that table, which trails the live source by the incremental refresh cadence) with a 60-second reorg buffer. Multi-hop router swaps are collapsed to one row per transaction. Dust is filtered via `live_trades_min_usd` var (default 1 USD); rows with unknown USD are kept. No LIMIT is applied — dashboards should add their own. For ingestion-level staleness, query `api_execution_live_trades_freshness` separately.
api_execution_live_trades.name: api_execution_live_trades

lag_seconds.description: Seconds between `newest_block_timestamp` and `server_now`. Higher = staler data.
lag_seconds.name: lag_seconds

newest_block_timestamp.description: Block timestamp of the most recent log in `execution_live.logs`.
newest_block_timestamp.name: newest_block_timestamp

server_now.description: ClickHouse server clock at query time.
server_now.name: server_now

api_execution_live_trades_freshness.description: One-row freshness indicator for the live-trades tab. Shows the newest ingested block timestamp in `execution_live.logs`, the server clock, and the lag in seconds. Intended for a "Data as of X, lag Y min" UI element — important because the tab is anchored on ingested-data time, not wall clock. Reads directly from the source so it keeps working when the feed itself is empty.
api_execution_live_trades_freshness.name: api_execution_live_trades_freshness

aggregator_share_pct.description: Percentage of trades where `tx.to_address` was labeled as a known aggregator/router.
aggregator_share_pct.name: aggregator_share_pct

multihop_share_pct.description: Percentage of trades with more than one swap event (router/aggregator paths).
multihop_share_pct.name: multihop_share_pct

trade_count.description: Number of transactions in the feed window.
trade_count.name: trade_count

volume_usd.description: Sum of `trade_usd` across the feed window. Rows with unknown USD contribute nothing.
volume_usd.name: volume_usd

api_execution_live_trades_stats.description: One-row summary for the live-trades tab header tiles. Counts trades, sums USD volume, counts unique traders, and computes aggregator/multi-hop shares. Reads from `api_execution_live_trades` so the tiles exactly match the population users see in the table (same window, same dust filter, same multi-hop collapse).
api_execution_live_trades_stats.name: api_execution_live_trades_stats

contracts_Swapr_v3_AlgebraPool_events_li.description: Plain view over `execution_live.logs` that decodes Swapr V3 Algebra Pool events in near-real-time. Same approach as `contracts_UniswapV3_Pool_events_live`: plain view (not MV) because cryo-live bulk attaches don't fire MV triggers, source pre-filtered to last 4h from source HWM for decode performance.
contracts_Swapr_v3_AlgebraPool_events_li.name: contracts_Swapr_v3_AlgebraPool_events_live

contracts_UniswapV3_Pool_events.description:
Pool addresses are dynamically selected from contracts_whitelist seed
based on whitelisted tokens (both token0 and token1 must be whitelisted).
contracts_UniswapV3_Pool_events.name: contracts_UniswapV3_Pool_events

contracts_UniswapV3_Pool_events_live.description: Plain view over `execution_live.logs` that decodes Uniswap V3 Pool events in near-real-time. Materialized as a view (not a materialized_view) because the cryo-live indexer populates logs via bulk part attaches that do not fire MV triggers. The source is pre-filtered to the last 4h (relative to the source HWM, not wall-clock) so the decode_logs scan stays fast instead of covering the full 48h TTL. The filter lands inside the macro's FROM clause — before the dedup window function and ABI join — which is the only position where ClickHouse can use it for partition/granule skipping.
contracts_UniswapV3_Pool_events_live.name: contracts_UniswapV3_Pool_events_live

contracts_circles_v2_PaymentGatewayFacto.description:
Decoded function calls for the Circles v2 PaymentGatewayFactory
contract. This model is decoded from `execution.traces` (not
`execution.transactions`) because createGateway is invoked through
a Safe in the ERC-4337 bundler path, so the Factory selector is
only visible one hop down in internal calls. decode_calls auto-
detects the traces source and emits an extra `trace_address`
column used in the unique_key.

abi_source_address.description: The Zodiac mastercopy address — where the ABI lives in event_signatures.csv. Lowercase, 0x-prefixed.
abi_source_address.name: abi_source_address

address.description: Module proxy address (lowercase, 0x-prefixed). Used as the address filter against execution.logs.
address.name: address

start_blocktime.description: Earliest block at which this proxy was enabled on a GP Safe.
start_blocktime.name: start_blocktime

contracts_gpay_modules_registry.description:
Cross-referenced registry of every Zodiac module proxy that is currently
or was ever enabled on a Gnosis Pay Safe.

Two evidence sources, joined on the proxy address:
  1. int_execution_safes_module_events — the Safe enabled this address
     as a module (filtered to GP Safes via int_execution_gpay_wallets).
  2. int_execution_zodiac_module_proxies — the address was deployed by
     the canonical Zodiac ModuleProxyFactory and points at one of the
     three known GP mastercopies.

The INNER JOIN means we drop any "enabled module" event that points to
an address we don't recognise as a Zodiac proxy of a GP-relevant type.
This is the high-quality registry that decode_logs consumes via
contract_address_ref to decode per-module events downstream.

Output schema matches the standard contracts_*_registry shape so
decode_logs picks up abi_source_address automatically.
contracts_gpay_modules_registry.name: contracts_gpay_modules_registry

abi_source_address.description: Singleton/mastercopy address. decode_logs joins event_signatures on (topic0, abi_source_address) so all Safe proxies share the singleton's decoded ABI from seeds/contracts_abi.csv.
abi_source_address.name: abi_source_address

address.description: The Safe proxy address (lowercase, 0x-prefixed).
address.name: address

contract_type.description: Always 'SafeProxy'. Pass to decode_logs as contract_type_filter.
contract_type.name: contract_type

is_dynamic.description: Always 1 (every Safe proxy was discovered on-chain).
is_dynamic.name: is_dynamic

start_blocktime.description: Earliest block at which this proxy can emit events (= int_execution_safes.block_timestamp).
start_blocktime.name: start_blocktime

contracts_safe_registry.description:
Registry consumed by decode_logs(contract_address_ref=ref('contracts_safe_registry')).
Maps each Safe proxy → the singleton whose ABI should be used to decode
its events.

Columns required by decode_logs:
  address              — the emitting contract (proxy)
  contract_type        — 'SafeProxy', so callers can pass contract_type_filter
  abi_source_address   — the singleton; decode_logs joins event_signatures
                         on (topic0, abi_source_address) to find the ABI
  is_dynamic           — 1 = discovered on-chain (all Safe proxies are)
  start_blocktime      — earliest block at which this proxy can emit events
  discovery_source     — provenance tag

Tiny model — one row per Safe (thousands to low millions). Materialized
as a table so decode_logs's compile-time get_columns_in_relation() works
the moment dbt runs the downstream events model.
contracts_safe_registry.name: contracts_safe_registry

trust_bucket.description: Bucket label for number of trusts (e.g. '1-5', '6-10', '100+').
trust_bucket.name: trust_bucket

fct_execution_circles_v2_trusts_distribu.description: Distribution histogram of trust degree (given/received) across avatars.
fct_execution_circles_v2_trusts_distribu.name: fct_execution_circles_v2_trusts_distribution

active_solvers.description: Number of distinct solvers that settled batches on this day.
active_solvers.name: active_solvers

cow_ratio.description: Share of batches that were pure CoW (peer-to-peer).
cow_ratio.name: cow_ratio

num_cow_batches.description: Batches settled via pure Coincidence of Wants (no AMM interactions).
num_cow_batches.name: num_cow_batches

total_tx_cost_native.description: Total gas cost in xDAI across all batches.
total_tx_cost_native.name: total_tx_cost_native

fct_execution_cow_daily.description: Daily CoW Protocol metrics: volume, trades, unique traders, fees, batch counts, CoW ratio (peer-to-peer matching share), and solver activity.
fct_execution_cow_daily.name: fct_execution_cow_daily

fct_execution_cow_solvers.description: Solver registry built from SolverAdded/SolverRemoved events. Shows the latest status (active/inactive) of each solver.
fct_execution_cow_solvers.name: fct_execution_cow_solvers

solver_is_active.description: Whether this solver is currently active in the AllowList.
solver_is_active.name: solver_is_active

fct_execution_cow_solvers_daily.description: Daily per-solver performance metrics: trades, volume, fees, batches, CoW batches, and gas costs.
fct_execution_cow_solvers_daily.name: fct_execution_cow_solvers_daily

api_fee_amount_raw.description: API fee in token atoms (sum across fee policies).
api_fee_amount_raw.name: api_fee_amount_raw

api_fee_token.description: Token address the API fee is denominated in.
api_fee_token.name: api_fee_token

fee_amount.description: On-chain fee in sell-token human units.
fee_amount.name: fee_amount

fee_amount_raw.description: On-chain fee atoms from Trade event (0 for 2024+).
fee_amount_raw.name: fee_amount_raw

fee_usd.description: Fee in USD. Derived from on-chain feeAmount when > 0 (pre-2024 trades), otherwise from the API fee scaled to USD via the trade's USD value.
fee_usd.name: fee_usd

fct_execution_cow_trades.description: Canonical CoW Protocol trades table. Joins on-chain trade data from int_execution_cow_trades with off-chain protocol fee data from stg_crawlers_data__cow_api_trade_fees (ingested from the CoW API). fee_usd logic: pre-2024 CoW used an explicit fee model (fee_amount_raw > 0 in the Trade event); 2024+ CoW switched to surplus-based fees (not visible in events) sourced from executedProtocolFees via /api/v2/trades; the transition period (mid-2023 through early 2024) is mixed — COALESCE picks whichever source is available per trade. fee_source identifies where the fee came from ('onchain', 'api', or NULL). NOTE — ClickHouse LEFT JOIN with join_use_nulls=0 (default) fills unmatched String columns with '' instead of NULL, so we check fee_token != '' rather than IS NOT NULL to correctly detect a real API match.
fct_execution_cow_trades.name: fct_execution_cow_trades

fct_execution_gnosis_app_activity_by_act.description: Daily activity counts, unique users, and USD by activity_kind. Feeds the Activity tab's stacked bar.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_activity_by_act.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_activity_by_action_daily

fct_execution_gnosis_app_churn_monthly.description:
Monthly churn & retention segments. Two scopes — 'Any' (any non-onboard
activity) and 'Swap' (swap_signed/swap_filled only). Segments: new,
retained (active M-1 and M), returning (active M and earlier-not-M-1),
churned (active M, not M+1). Port of fct_execution_gpay_churn_monthly.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_churn_monthly.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_churn_monthly

fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_by_.description:
Daily Gnosis App → Gnosis Pay TopUp activity, derived from
int_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups. Backing fact for
api_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_by_token_daily.

Grain: (date, token_bought_symbol). The dashboard can aggregate across
tokens or filter client-side (e.g. only EURe, only USDC). n_ga_users and
n_gp_wallets are distinct counts within the slice.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_by_.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_by_token_daily

fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_coh.description:
First-TopUp cohort × subsequent-TopUp activity retention. cohort_month
= month of user's first TopUp. retention_pct = users_still_topping_up /
cohort_initial × 100. Same shape as fct_execution_gpay_cashback_cohort_retention_monthly.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_coh.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_cohort_monthly

fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_mon.description: Monthly topup counts and USD volume (aggregated across tokens).
fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_mon.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_monthly

fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_wee.description: Weekly topup counts and USD volume (aggregated across tokens).
fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_wee.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_weekly

n_ga_wallets_cumulative.description: Running total of ever-GA-owned GP wallets up to and including this date.
n_ga_wallets_cumulative.name: n_ga_wallets_cumulative

n_ga_wallets_new.description: GP wallets that became GA-owned for the first time on this date.
n_ga_wallets_new.name: n_ga_wallets_new

fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_wallets_da.description:
Daily cumulative count of Gnosis Pay wallets controlled by a Gnosis App
user, split by onboarding class. Backing fact table for
api_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_wallets_daily.

  onboarded_via_ga : the very first owner of the GP Safe was a GA user.
                     User went from zero → Gnosis Pay via the Gnosis App.
  imported         : GA user appears in the owner set after creation.
                     User already had Gnosis Pay and later linked their GA.

V1 tracks the date a GP wallet first gained a GA owner (first_ga_owner_at)
and its cumulative count over time. Removals (GA user subsequently removed
from the owner set) are NOT subtracted here — dashboards can read
is_currently_ga_owned from int_execution_gpay_ga_wallets for a
point-in-time currently-owned snapshot.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_wallets_da.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_wallets_daily

n_new_payers.description: Distinct payers whose FIRST buy on this offer was this day.
n_new_payers.name: n_new_payers

fct_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_buy.description:
Cumulative marketplace buys and distinct payers per offer, computed as
running window sums over a dense (offer × date) spine so series are
continuous. Mirrors Dune query_6679414's Cumulative Buys / Cumulative
Users window aggregates.

n_buys / n_new_payers on each row is the per-day delta; cumulative_buys
/ cumulative_payers is the running total for that offer.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_buy.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_buys_cumulative_daily

n_payers.description: Distinct GA payer addresses on this (date, offer_name).
n_payers.name: n_payers

volume_token.description: Sum of amount (CRC human units) across buys on this day.
volume_token.name: volume_token

fct_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_buy.description:
Daily marketplace buys per offer, derived from
int_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_payments. Grain (date, offer_name).
fct_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_buy.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_buys_daily

fct_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_off.description:
Latest per-offer totals — one row per curated offer with lifetime
n_buys, distinct payers, and first/last buy timestamps. Includes
offers with zero buys (LEFT JOIN) so the dashboard leaderboard shows
new listings too.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_off.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_offers_latest

fct_execution_gnosis_app_retention_by_ac.description:
Retention split by activity_kind (swap_filled / topup / marketplace_buy /
circles_trust / etc.). Uses the GLOBAL onboard cohort_month as the
anchor so the denominator is consistent across action types — the
numerator counts users who did that specific action in activity_month.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_retention_by_ac.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_retention_by_action_monthly

fct_execution_gnosis_app_retention_month.description:
Cohort retention grid. cohort_month = month of user's 'onboard' row
(first-ever heuristic hit). activity_month = month of any subsequent
non-onboard activity. retention_pct = users_in_activity_month /
cohort_initial_size × 100. Matches the fct_execution_gpay_retention_monthly
shape; port for GA.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_retention_month.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_retention_monthly

fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_by_pair_d.description:
Daily swap activity sliced by token pair (sold_symbol → bought_symbol).
pair is a pre-joined label for dashboard use; raw symbols are exposed
separately for downstream joins.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_by_pair_d.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_by_pair_daily

fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_by_solver.description: Daily swap activity sliced by solver (filled trades only).
fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_by_solver.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_by_solver_daily

date.description: Date of the PreSignature event (= when the user signed in-app).
date.name: date

n_filled_unpriced.description: Filled orders lacking a USD price (debug signal).
n_filled_unpriced.name: n_filled_unpriced

n_orders.description: Distinct order_uid values (≈ n_swaps; lower if an order was re-signed).
n_orders.name: n_orders

n_swappers.description: Distinct GA user (taker) addresses with ≥1 signed order that day.
n_swappers.name: n_swappers

n_swaps.description: Count of PreSignature-signed orders (= Dune count).
n_swaps.name: n_swaps

n_swaps_filled.description: Signed orders that subsequently had at least one Trade fill.
n_swaps_filled.name: n_swaps_filled

n_swaps_unfilled.description: Signed orders with no matching Trade fill.
n_swaps_unfilled.name: n_swaps_unfilled

volume_usd_filled.description: Sum of amount_usd across filled orders.
volume_usd_filled.name: volume_usd_filled

volume_usd_priced.description: Sum of amount_usd restricted to orders with a non-null USD price.
volume_usd_priced.name: volume_usd_priced

fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_daily.description:
Daily Gnosis App swap activity, derived from int_execution_gnosis_app_swaps.
Backing fact for api_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_daily.

Dune-parity semantic: one swap = one PreSignature event (= one signed
order intent) via the Cometh bundler. Orders pre-signed but never filled
are still counted as n_swaps; volume_usd reflects only filled orders.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_daily.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_daily

fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_monthly.description: Monthly swap counts and filled USD volume.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_monthly.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_monthly

fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_weekly.description: Weekly swap counts and filled USD volume (bucket = ISO Mon-start).
fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_weekly.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_swaps_weekly

fct_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_cla.description:
Daily token-offer claims sliced by specific offer (nextOffer address).
offer_token_symbol surfaces which token was sold; offer_price_in_crc is
the sticker price set when the offer was created (any() — stable per offer).
fct_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_cla.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_claims_by_offer_daily

fct_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_cla.description:
First-claim cohort × subsequent-claim activity retention. cohort_month
= month of user's first token-offer claim. retention_pct = users still
claiming in the given activity_month / cohort_initial × 100. Same shape
as fct_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups_cohort_monthly.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_cla.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_claims_cohort_monthly

fct_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_cla.description:
Daily Gnosis App token-offer claim activity, derived from
int_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_claims. One row per date.
A claim = a GA user paying CRC to receive an offer_token (GNO today)
via a Cometh-relayed tx to the ERC-4337 EntryPoint.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_cla.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_claims_daily

fct_execution_gnosis_app_users_daily.description:
Daily user counts. new_users = distinct addresses whose first-ever
activity hit was on this date; active_users = distinct addresses with
any non-onboard activity; returning_users = active today AND had any
activity in the prior 7 days; reactivated_users = active today,
inactive for prior 30 days, but active before that. Dense calendar
spine so cumulative_users is always continuous.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_users_daily.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_users_daily

fct_execution_gnosis_app_users_monthly.description: Monthly cohort of users. Returning = active this month AND previous month. Reactivated = active this month, inactive for prior 2 months, active before that.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_users_monthly.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_users_monthly

fct_execution_gnosis_app_users_weekly.description: Weekly cohort of users (ISO Mon-start). Returning = active this week AND previous week. Reactivated = active this week, inactive for prior 4 weeks.
fct_execution_gnosis_app_users_weekly.name: fct_execution_gnosis_app_users_weekly

lvr_apr_7d.description: Annualised 7-day trailing LVR (%). Always <= 0 (negative = loss). Represents the cost of adverse selection from arbitrage swaps, with fee revenue stripped from raw swap flows.
lvr_apr_7d.name: lvr_apr_7d

fct_execution_pools_il_daily.description: Pool-level Loss Versus Rebalancing (LVR) from actual swap flows. LVR measures the cost LPs pay to arbitrageurs — the value extracted via swap rebalancing. Sign convention: negative = loss (arbitrage extracted value), positive = gain. Swap events already embed the concentrated-liquidity math executed by the pool contract, so this captures V3 amplification without per-position tracking. Each day's swap flows are valued at that day's token prices, then summed over the 7-day window. lvr_apr_7d = (swap_flow_usd_7d − fees_usd_7d) / avg_tvl_7d × (365/7) × 100. Fees are subtracted because swap event amounts include fees on the input side.
fct_execution_pools_il_daily.name: fct_execution_pools_il_daily

for every role it plays across the sectors we track (Safe, GPay,
Circles, DeFi pools/lending, validator withdrawal, Dune labels).
Powers the Graph Explorer's node-kind badges and auto-discovery of
applicable graph profiles when a user seeds from a bare address.

int_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups.description:
Gnosis App → Gnosis Pay TopUps.

A TopUp = a CoW Protocol trade initiated by a GA user whose bought token
ends up in a GP wallet in the same settlement tx.

Detection:
  1. Filter int_execution_cow_trades to taker ∈ GA users.
  2. In the same transaction_hash, find an ERC-20 Transfer of the
     trade's token_bought_address whose recipient is a known GP wallet.

Per design decision (April 2026), we do NOT filter by token_sold = CRC —
the original Slack heuristic suggested CRC-only but the Dune dashboard
already shows TopUps in many tokens. We expose token_sold_symbol so the
dashboard can filter client-side.

Scope note: uses int_execution_transfers_whitelisted_raw for the payout
side, which only covers whitelisted tokens. The vast majority of GP
top-ups are into EURe / USDC (whitelisted), so this covers the
practical use-case. TopUps into non-whitelisted tokens would be
missed — rare and acceptable for V1.
int_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups.name: int_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups

int_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_wallets.description:
Gnosis Pay wallets (Safes) that have been or currently are controlled
by a Gnosis App user, via the Safe's Zodiac **Delay Module**.

Heuristic (Hugo's Dune query 6675111, Bernardo review April 2026):

  The Delay Module bound to each GP Safe has its OWN access control —
  Zodiac's enableModule(address)/disableModule(address) functions,
  which emit EnabledModule/DisabledModule events. Any address enabled
  as a module on the Delay can queue transactions on the GP Safe.

  A GP wallet is "on Gnosis App" iff a GA user address has been
  enabled (net +1) as a module on its Delay.

Events scanned (from execution.logs):

  EnabledModule  topic0 = 0xecdf3a3e…f8440 (module addr in DATA field)
  DisabledModule topic0 = 0xaab4fa2b…54276 (module addr in DATA field)

Mapping Delay Module → GP Safe comes from
int_execution_gpay_safe_modules.contract_type = 'DelayModule'.

Incremental: on each run, scans only delay modules that had an
EnabledModule/DisabledModule event in the last 30 days (or since
previous-run watermark), then recomputes the FULL event history for
just those modules. delete+insert on unique_key=pay_wallet keeps
unchanged rows from previous runs. Full-refresh scans everything back
to 2023-06-01.

Onboarding classes (per Daniel's dashboard request):

  onboarded_via_ga : the very first module ever enabled on this Delay
                     is a GA user — user went from zero → Gnosis Pay
                     via Gnosis App.
  imported         : a GA user was added as a module later — user
                     already had Gnosis Pay, then linked their GA Safe.

One row per GP Safe. A Safe with multiple GA modules enabled is still
counted once.
int_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_wallets.name: int_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_wallets

int_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_off.description:
Gnosis App marketplace offers — one row per non-excluded PaymentGateway
created via Circles v2 PaymentGatewayFactory.

Sources & join:
  1. createGateway calls from contracts_circles_v2_PaymentGatewayFactory_calls
     (carries the `name` string input as offer_name).
  2. GatewayCreated events from contracts_circles_v2_PaymentGatewayFactory_events
     (carries the resulting `gateway` address and `owner`).
  3. Joined on transaction_hash — createGateway emits GatewayCreated in
     the same tx — to pair (offer_name ↔ gateway_address).

Exclusion: seeds/gnosis_app_marketplace_offers_excluded.csv. Rows match
on offer_name OR gateway_address (empty string = "ignore this column for
this row"). Dynamic-inclusion semantic: every new offer automatically
appears in this model unless explicitly blocklisted — ops team curates
the seed when a test gateway needs to be hidden.
int_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_off.name: int_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_offers

int_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_pay.description:
Gnosis App marketplace payments — one row per PaymentReceived event
from Circles v2 PaymentGatewayFactory that satisfies ALL of:

  1. Event owner (`payer`) ∈ GA users (int_execution_gnosis_app_users_current)
  2. Outer tx.from ∈ Cometh Relayers V4 (seed gnosis_app_relayers)
  3. Outer tx.to = ERC-4337 EntryPoint v0.7 (0x…7727de22…)
  4. Event `gateway` ∈ curated offer set (int_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_offers)

Mirrors Dune query_6679414. `amount` is the CRC-scaled (1e18) float
form of `decoded_params['amount']` — no USD pricing yet (TBD once the
Circles token price feed is wired).
int_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_pay.name: int_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_payments

int_execution_gnosis_app_swaps.description:
Gnosis App swaps — one row per CoW order pre-signed by a GA user through
the Cometh ERC-4337 bundler. Matches the Dune semantic: the anchor is
the PreSignature event, NOT the Trade fill. Orders that are pre-signed
but never filled (cancelled, expired, solver mismatch) are still counted
as app activity.

Strict heuristic (all must hold for a row to appear):
  1. GPv2Settlement.PreSignature event with signed=true
  2. PreSignature tx.to  = ERC-4337 EntryPoint v0.7 (0x…7727de22…)
  3. PreSignature tx.from ∈ Cometh Relayers V4 (seed gnosis_app_relayers)
  4. PreSignature owner   ∈ GA users
                            (int_execution_gnosis_app_users_current)

Trade enrichment (tokens, amounts, USD, solver) is a LEFT JOIN — NULL
for unfilled orders. `was_filled` surfaces this as a boolean. Partial
fills produce multiple Trade rows for one PreSignature; for the LEFT
JOIN we pick the first fill (argMin on block_timestamp) and aggregate
amounts via sum so one PreSignature = one output row.

Grain: (transaction_hash, log_index) of the PreSignature event. Row
count on this model = count of GA-app signed orders.
int_execution_gnosis_app_swaps.name: int_execution_gnosis_app_swaps

int_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_cla.description:
Gnosis App token-offer claims — one row per `OfferClaimed` event emitted
by any ERC20TokenOfferCycle where the claim was:

  1. The caller (`account`) is a Gnosis App user
     (int_execution_gnosis_app_users_current).
  2. The outer tx was relayed by a Cometh V4 bundler
     (seeds/gnosis_app_relayers, is_active=1).
  3. The outer tx.to is the ERC-4337 EntryPoint v0.7
     (0x0000000071727de22e5e9d8baf0edac6f37da032).

Mirrors the Dune reference query 6582075. Joined to
int_execution_gnosis_app_token_offers for offer/cycle/token metadata, and
to int_execution_token_prices_daily to price `received` (offer_token) in
USD. `spent` is in CRC base units; no USD pricing yet (CRC price feed TBD).
int_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_cla.name: int_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_claims

token_price_in_crc_raw.description: Raw uint256 CRC price per offer_token unit (before decimals scaling).
token_price_in_crc_raw.name: token_price_in_crc_raw

int_execution_gnosis_app_token_offers.description:
Gnosis App token-offer registry — one row per `NextOffer` instance emitted
by the Circles v2 ERC20TokenOfferCycle contracts. Each cycle (there are 3
deployed — v1/v2/v3) periodically emits `NextOfferCreated` for a new sale
instance with a fixed CRC price and per-user CRC cap; users then pay CRC
to receive the cycle's `offerToken` (GNO today).

Sources & join:
  1. CycleConfiguration event on the cycle address — carries offerToken,
     admin, offersStart, offerDuration.
  2. NextOfferCreated event on the same cycle address — carries the
     specific offer (nextOffer) address, tokenPriceInCRC, offerLimitInCRC.
  3. Joined on (contract_address = cycle_address) so each offer is
     enriched with its parent cycle's configuration.
  4. NextOfferTokensDeposited aggregated by offer for supply side.

Token metadata (symbol, decimals) comes from tokens_whitelist, keyed on
the lowercase offer_token_address.
int_execution_gnosis_app_token_offers.name: int_execution_gnosis_app_token_offers

address.description: GA user address (0x-prefixed, lowercase).
address.name: address

int_execution_gnosis_app_user_activity_d.description:
Foundation table for all Gnosis App user-activity analytics — one row
per (date, address, activity_kind). Every downstream cohort, retention,
churn, DAU/WAU/MAU, and activity-by-action mart derives from this table.

activity_kind enum:
  onboard                 — first-ever heuristic hit for this address
                             (from int_execution_gnosis_app_users_current.
                             first_seen_at). Exactly one row per user.
                             Anchors the cohort_month for retention.
  circles_register_human,
  circles_invite_human,
  circles_trust,
  circles_profile_update,
  circles_metri_fee,
  safe_invitation_module  — raw heuristic rows from
                             int_execution_gnosis_app_user_events.
  swap_signed             — PreSignature events (all rows in
                             int_execution_gnosis_app_swaps).
  swap_filled             — subset with was_filled = true.
  topup                   — GA→GP TopUps
                             (int_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_topups).
  marketplace_buy         — GA marketplace purchases
                             (int_execution_gnosis_app_marketplace_payments).
  token_offer_claim       — GA token-offer claims (e.g. CRC → GNO discount)
                             (int_execution_gnosis_app_token_offer_claims).

amount_usd populated for swap_filled, topup, and token_offer_claim
(USD on the *received* side only). marketplace buys carry NULL until the
Circles token price feed is wired.
int_execution_gnosis_app_user_activity_d.name: int_execution_gnosis_app_user_activity_daily

address.description: The Gnosis App user address (avatar / inviter / truster / Safe, depending on the rule). Lowercase, 0x-prefixed — the same form that decode_logs writes for decoded_params address values and that stg_mixpanel_ga__events sees for wallet-identified users. Storing in this form means the cross-domain pseudonym join lines up exactly without any per-side normalization.
address.name: address

block_timestamp.description: UTC timestamp of the block in which the action happened.

heuristic_kind.name: heuristic_kind

transaction_hash.name: transaction_hash

int_execution_gnosis_app_user_events.description:
Long-form heuristic event log for Gnosis App user identification.

Six rules, all keyed off the same chokepoint: a Cometh ERC-4337
bundler (one of three relayer addresses in
seeds/gnosis_app_relayers.csv) sent the outer transaction to the
canonical EntryPoint v0.7. Any account that appears as a parameter
inside such a transaction is, by construction, acting through Gnosis
App for that action.

Rules:
  safe_invitation_module  — Safe created in the same Cometh tx that
                            enabled the Circles InvitationModule
                            (0x00738aca…). The Safe address is the
                            user.
  circles_metri_fee       — CRC ERC-1155 transfer to the Metri fee
                            receiver (0x97FD8f78…) inside a Cometh tx.
  circles_register_human  — Hub.RegisterHuman event inside a Cometh
                            tx. decoded_params['avatar'] is the user.
  circles_invite_human    — Same RegisterHuman event with a non-zero
                            decoded_params['inviter'] — the inviter
                            is the user.
  circles_trust           — Hub.Trust event inside a Cometh tx.
                            decoded_params['truster'] is the user.
  circles_profile_update  — NameRegistry.UpdateMetadataDigest event
                            inside a Cometh tx.
                            decoded_params['avatar'] is the user.

Address columns are stored lowercase WITH the 0x prefix to match the
rest of the dbt outputs and the format that decode_logs produces for
decoded_params address values. Rule 1 reads from int_execution_safes
(which is already 0x-prefixed) and rules 2-6 read from
contracts_circles_v2_*_events.decoded_params (also 0x-prefixed).

Privacy: this is the source-of-truth intermediate for sector
membership. Raw addresses are kept here because they are required
for the snapshot argMax and for the cross-reference with
int_execution_safes. The pseudonymization boundary is in
int_execution_gnosis_app_user_identities, one model downstream.
int_execution_gnosis_app_user_events.name: int_execution_gnosis_app_user_events

heuristic_kinds.name: heuristic_kinds

last_seen_at.name: last_seen_at

n_distinct_heuristics.description: Confidence proxy — number of distinct rules fired.
n_distinct_heuristics.name: n_distinct_heuristics

user_pseudonym.description: Keyed pseudonym (UInt64) of the Gnosis App user address. Joinable to stg_mixpanel_ga__events.user_id_hash on equality, since both sides use the same pseudonymize_address macro and the same salt.
user_pseudonym.name: user_pseudonym

int_execution_gnosis_app_user_identities.description:
Pseudonymization boundary for the Gnosis App sector.

Every address from the snapshot is hashed via pseudonymize_address
before flowing into any marts model. Upstream addresses are already
0x-prefixed (the convention used everywhere in the dbt outputs and
the same form stg_mixpanel_ga__events ingests for wallet-identified
Mixpanel distinct_ids), so the hash on both sides of the cross-domain
join is computed over identical inputs.

Output: one row per Gnosis App user pseudonym, joinable to
stg_mixpanel_ga__events.user_id_hash on equality.

No raw_address column is exposed — for v1 we drop it entirely. If a
later cross-product join (e.g. GP × Gnosis App) needs raw addresses
for an internal-only intermediate, that join lives upstream of this
model, not downstream.
int_execution_gnosis_app_user_identities.name: int_execution_gnosis_app_user_identities

int_execution_gnosis_app_users_current.description:

Gnosis App user.

Cheap to fully rebuild every run; no incremental needed.
int_execution_gnosis_app_users_current.name: int_execution_gnosis_app_users_current

int_execution_gpay_allowances_current.description:
Current allowance state per Gnosis Pay Safe.

Replays SetAllowance events from int_execution_gpay_roles_events and
keeps the latest row per (roles_module, allowance_key). Joins back to
the GP Safe via int_execution_gpay_safe_modules.

Columns are projected as Strings because the underlying decoded_params
values are Strings (decode_logs returns Map(String, String)). Cast to
numeric types in downstream marts when needed — keeping them as
Strings here avoids precision loss for uint128 balance values that
exceed UInt64.

`refill` is the canonical "daily limit" number — the amount the
allowance refills by once every `period` seconds.

Daily count of TransactionAdded events per GP Safe — the
privacy-respecting "user did something admin-y today" signal.

TransactionAdded fires whenever the original setup owner queues a
delayed transaction (withdraw, key rotation, owner swap, …) via the
Delay module. We don't decode WHAT they queued — just the count and
timing. Useful for the Mixpanel bridge as a per-user activity dimension.

Joined to int_execution_gpay_safe_modules to map the Delay module
proxy back to its owning GP Safe.

int_execution_gpay_delay_events.description:
Decoded events emitted by the Zodiac Delay Module proxies attached to
GP Safes. Includes:

  DelaySetup       — module enabled and bound to a Safe
  TransactionAdded — admin action queued by the original setup owner
                     (the privacy-respecting "user did something admin-y"
                     signal — single most useful Delay event for analytics)
  TxCooldownSet    — cooldown configuration mutation
  TxExpirationSet  — expiration configuration mutation
  TxNonceSet       — nonce reset

decode_logs filters logs.address to (SELECT address FROM
contracts_gpay_modules_registry WHERE contract_type='DelayModule') and
joins event_signatures on the delayModMastercopy address — that's the
abi_source_address column the registry exposes.

int_execution_gpay_roles_events.description:
Decoded events from the Zodiac Roles v2 module proxies attached to GP
Safes. Includes the events Gnosis Pay actually mutates:

  RolesModSetup    — module enabled and bound to a Safe
  AssignRoles      — spender delegate assignment.
                     roleKeys (bytes32[]) and memberOf (bool[]) are
                     parallel arrays — we ARRAY JOIN them so each row
                     is one (member, role_key, is_member) triple.
  SetDefaultRole   — default role assignment for a delegate
  SetAllowance     — allowance / daily-limit creation or update
  ConsumeAllowance — allowance burn-down on a successful card spend

decode_logs returns decoded_params as a Map(String, String). Array
params are stored as JSON array strings (toJSONString of the decoded
array). ClickHouse JSONExtract* functions are 1-indexed.
int_execution_gpay_roles_events.name: int_execution_gpay_roles_events

gp_safe.description: The Gnosis Pay Safe address (lowercase, 0x-prefixed). Treated as a smart-account identifier rather than PII.
gp_safe.name: gp_safe

user_pseudonym.description: Keyed pseudonym (UInt64) of the underlying address. Joinable to stg_mixpanel_ga__events.user_id_hash on equality.
user_pseudonym.name: user_pseudonym

int_execution_gpay_safe_identities.description:
PURPOSE
-------
This model is the privacy boundary for the Gnosis Pay ↔ Mixpanel
bridge. It produces a lookup table from `user_pseudonym` (a salted
keyed hash of an on-chain address) to `gp_safe` + `identity_role`,
so the downstream Mixpanel fact model can join on
`mp.user_id_hash = id.user_pseudonym` without ever touching a raw
address.

Everything upstream (int_execution_safes_owner_events,
int_execution_gpay_spender_delegates_current, int_execution_gpay_wallets)
still holds raw addresses because the on-chain pipeline needs them
for joins. Everything downstream (fct_mixpanel_ga_gpay_users,
fct_mixpanel_ga_gpay_crossdomain_daily) only sees `user_pseudonym`
and `gp_safe`. This model is the one place where the
`pseudonymize_address` macro is applied; the macro is the same one
stg_mixpanel_ga__events uses at ingest, and the salt is the same
env var (CEREBRO_PII_SALT), so the join keys are identical on both
sides.

WHY THREE IDENTITY ROLES
------------------------
The same person shows up in Mixpanel as one of THREE different
identifiers depending on which GP frontend flow created their
session. Picking only one of them collapses the match rate to near
zero. The three roles, in order of likely match volume:

  initial_owner — the EOA the user signed in with via passkey or
                  wallet during onboarding. After the GP setup
                  ceremony, ownership of the Safe is transferred to
                  the inaccessible sentinel 0x...0002 and this EOA
                  only retains access through the Delay module. But
                  it is still whatever wallet the user authenticated
                  with, so it is the most likely value the GP
                  frontend passes to mixpanel.identify(...). Pulled
                  from int_execution_safes_owner_events where
                  event_kind='safe_setup'.

  delegate      — a spender delegate EOA assigned via Roles v2
                  AssignRoles events. These are usually backend-
                  managed card-flow keys, but a few flows may
                  identify with them. Pulled from
                  int_execution_gpay_spender_delegates_current.

  safe_self     — the Safe smart-account address itself. Some GP
                  frontend flows call mixpanel.identify(safeAddress)
                  instead of identify(eoaAddress). Pulled directly
                  from int_execution_gpay_wallets.

Row grain: one row per (gp_safe, identity_role, user_pseudonym). A
Safe with two initial owners and three delegates produces 6 rows
(2 + 3 + 1 self). A single user_pseudonym can appear under multiple
gp_safe values if the same person owns multiple GP cards.

PRIVACY INVARIANT
-----------------
Every column derived from a wallet address is hashed via
pseudonymize_address. The output schema contains user_pseudonym
(irreversible without the salt) and gp_safe (treated as a smart-
account identifier rather than PII — flip if that classification
ever changes). Raw EOA addresses never reach this model's output.
int_execution_gpay_safe_identities.name: int_execution_gpay_safe_identities

enabled_at.name: enabled_at

module_proxy_address.name: module_proxy_address

int_execution_gpay_safe_modules.description:
Current module topology per Gnosis Pay Safe.

Replays int_execution_safes_module_events filtered to GP Safes, keeps
the latest event per (safe, module_proxy) via argMax, and drops rows
whose latest event is `disabled_module`. The remaining rows are joined
to contracts_gpay_modules_registry to get the module type
(DelayModule / RolesModule / SpenderModule).

Expected row count: ≈3 × |int_execution_gpay_wallets|. Anything other than 3
per Safe indicates a partially-onboarded GP Safe (worth investigating).
int_execution_gpay_safe_modules.name: int_execution_gpay_safe_modules

int_execution_gpay_spend_activity_daily.description:
Daily card-spend activity per GP Safe.

Source: int_execution_gpay_spender_events filtered to event_name='Spend'.
The Spend event already carries `account` (the debited Safe), so we
don't need to join through int_execution_gpay_safe_modules — the
account address IS the gp_safe. We INNER JOIN to int_execution_gpay_wallets to
guard against any cross-Safe Spend events that wouldn't be GP-related.

Aggregates count of spends and number of distinct assets/receivers.
Amounts are not summed because they're per-asset and per-decimals; a
follow-up model can do the USD valuation join.
int_execution_gpay_spend_activity_daily.name: int_execution_gpay_spend_activity_daily

role_key.description: The Roles v2 role key under which the delegate is authorized.
role_key.name: role_key

int_execution_gpay_spender_delegates_cur.description:
Current spender-delegate set per Gnosis Pay Safe.

Replays AssignRoles events from int_execution_gpay_roles_events with
argMax per (roles_module, member, role_key) and keeps only the
combinations whose latest `is_member` is 1. Joins back to the GP Safe
via int_execution_gpay_safe_modules (RolesModule proxy → owning Safe).

A Safe with a Roles module that has authorized 2 spender keys produces
2 rows here. A Safe with no delegates produces 0 rows. The delegate
address is the EOA the GP card-flow infrastructure uses to trigger
spends within the user's allowance.
int_execution_gpay_spender_delegates_cur.name: int_execution_gpay_spender_delegates_current

int_execution_gpay_spender_events.description:
Decoded events from the Gnosis Pay Spender module proxies attached to
GP Safes. The Spender is GP's custom final gatekeeper for card-spend
authorization (deployed and controlled by GP's settlement infra).

Events of interest:

  Spend(asset, account, receiver, amount)
      — THE event. Fired on every successful card transaction. The
        headline analytics signal for GP card activity.
            asset    — token being spent (ERC-20 contract address)
            account  — the GP Safe being debited
            receiver — settlement / merchant-acquirer destination
            amount   — token base units (string; could be large)

  AvatarSet(previousAvatar, newAvatar)
      — Spender bound to a Safe (or rebound). The newAvatar is the
        GP Safe this Spender module belongs to. Setup signal.

  TargetSet(previousTarget, newTarget)
      — The Spender's target contract changed.

  EnabledModule / DisabledModule
      — Sub-modules of the Spender enabled or disabled (the Spender
        itself acts as a module manager for its own sub-stack).

  ExecutionFromModuleSuccess / ExecutionFromModuleFailure
      — A sub-module forwarded a call through the Spender.

  OwnershipTransferred / Initialized
      — Standard ownable / proxy lifecycle.

  HashExecuted / HashInvalidated
      — Signed-message tracking. The parameter is an unnamed bytes32
        so we cannot pluck it by key — we just record the event fired
        and leave the bytes32 in the raw decoded_params for downstream
        consumers that need it.
int_execution_gpay_spender_events.name: int_execution_gpay_spender_events

block_timestamp.description: Timestamp of the event that added (or set up) this owner — i.e. the most recent SafeSetup or AddedOwner event for this (pay_wallet, owner) pair. Different semantics from earlier versions of this model, which stored the Safe's creation time here.
block_timestamp.name: block_timestamp

int_execution_gpay_wallet_owners.description: Current owner snapshot for Gnosis Pay Safes. Thin filter over int_execution_safes_current_owners (the generic Safe ownership model) so post-setup owner mutations (AddedOwner / RemovedOwner / ChangedThreshold) are reflected, not just the creation-time SafeSetup snapshot. One row per (pay_wallet, owner) pair.
int_execution_gpay_wallet_owners.name: int_execution_gpay_wallet_owners

that is NOT the real human admin; use the Delay-module binding in
int_execution_gnosis_app_gpay_wallets.first_ga_owner_address for
that path instead.

int_execution_gpay_wallets.description:
Gnosis Pay Safes derived from on-chain events. A Safe qualifies if it
has emitted SafeSetup (so it exists on-chain) AND has ever sent a
whitelisted ERC-20 transfer to the Gnosis Pay spender
0x4822521E6135CD2599199c83Ea35179229A172EE, excluding addresses in the
gpay_operational_wallets seed. Replaces the crawler-sourced
stg_gpay__wallets model.

int_execution_lending_aave_diffs_daily.description: Daily per-user scaled balance deltas for Gnosis lending markets (Aave V3 and SparkLend). Computes scaled deltas from Pool events (Supply, Withdraw, Repay with aTokens, LiquidationCall where receiveAToken=false) and aToken BalanceTransfer events, aggregated per (date, protocol, user, reserve). Scaled math uses exact UInt256 WadRayMath and each pool action is paired with the ReserveDataUpdated snapshot at the same log_index position within its (protocol, transaction). LiquidationCall rows where receiveAToken=true are intentionally excluded because the collateral movement is already captured by the BalanceTransfer(borrower -> liquidator) event.

int_execution_pools_dex_liquidity_events.description: Individual LP add/remove/collect events across all protocols on Gnosis Chain. One row per (event, token) — not pivoted to wide format because Balancer pools can hold 2–8 tokens, making a fixed-width schema incorrect. Each protocol's event decoding lives in its own staging model (stg_pools__dex_liquidity_*). This model unions them, enriches with token metadata, adds USD prices (ASOF join), and joins execution.transactions for tx_from/tx_to. The tx join uses an IN subquery so ClickHouse builds a small hash set of event tx hashes and probes the large transactions table, rather than loading the full right side into memory. Protocols: Uniswap V3, Swapr V3, Balancer V2, Balancer V3. event_type values: 'mint' (add), 'burn' (remove), or 'collect' (claim fees; V3 only). amount_raw is always positive; event_type conveys direction. Includes V3 tick range (tick_lower/tick_upper) and signed liquidity delta for position-level tracking and in-range analysis. When called with start_month/end_month vars each protocol model filters to that month window; otherwise falls back to the incremental filter.
int_execution_pools_dex_liquidity_events.name: int_execution_pools_dex_liquidity_events

int_execution_pools_dex_trades_raw.description: Raw DEX swap events across all protocols on Gnosis Chain. One row per swap with token metadata (symbol, decimals, human amounts) but no USD prices — price enrichment lives in int_execution_pools_dex_trades. Each protocol's event decoding lives in its own staging model for easy addition of new protocols. Protocols: Uniswap V3 (stg_pools__dex_trades_uniswap_v3), Swapr V3 (stg_pools__dex_trades_swapr_v3), Balancer V2 (stg_pools__dex_trades_balancer_v2), Balancer V3 (stg_pools__dex_trades_balancer_v3). Taker: for Uniswap V3 and Swapr V3, `taker` is the Swap event recipient (correct even when routed through a contract). For Balancer V2/V3, `taker` is NULL because the Swap event does not include a recipient field. When called with start_month/end_month vars each protocol model filters to that month window; otherwise falls back to the incremental filter.
int_execution_pools_dex_trades_raw.name: int_execution_pools_dex_trades_raw

int_execution_pools_dex_trades_tx_contex.description: Thin lookup mapping swap transaction hashes to sender (tx_from) and recipient (tx_to). Only contains transactions that appear in int_execution_pools_dex_trades_raw, keeping the table small (~300–400K distinct hashes per month, ~80 MB). Used by int_execution_pools_dex_trades as a lightweight right-side join instead of hitting the full execution.transactions table inside the same query that also runs ASOF price joins.
int_execution_pools_dex_trades_tx_contex.name: int_execution_pools_dex_trades_tx_context

int_execution_pools_fees_daily.description: Daily accrued pool fees and trading volume at pool×token grain for Uniswap V3 and Swapr V3 pools, computed from Swap + Flash events (gross fees; independent of Collect/claims). V3 swap/flash deltas are sourced from staging models (stg_pools__uniswap_v3_events, stg_pools__swapr_v3_events) which handle int256 two's-complement decoding. Fees are applied in ppm (1e6 denominator). Volume = gross incoming token amount per swap; flash loans contribute 0 volume. Swapr V3 (Algebra) fees are dynamic via Fee events — the latest fee is applied as-of each swap, with swaps before the first Fee event backfilled using the pool's first observed fee.
int_execution_pools_fees_daily.name: int_execution_pools_fees_daily

Separates reserves from unclaimed fees: reserve_amount = Mint/Burn liquidity + swap deltas net of fees; fee_amount = swap fee income − collected fees + flash loan fees. int_execution_safes.description:
Gnosis Safe deployments on Gnosis Chain.

A Safe is deployed by the SafeProxyFactory as a minimal proxy that
delegatecalls into a singleton/mastercopy. The constructor immediately
delegatecalls setup(...) on the singleton — that's how we identify a
Safe trace: a delegatecall to a known singleton whose calldata starts
with one of the historical setup() selectors. The singleton-to-version
map lives in seeds/safe_singletons.csv.

Covers every Safe version (0.1.0 through 1.4.1, including the Circles
1.1.1 fork and the L2 variants), including pre-v1.1.0 Safes that never
emitted SafeSetup.

Source: execution.traces. We use dedup_source(..., pre_filter=...) so
the time-range and selector filters land BEFORE the ROW_NUMBER window
function and ClickHouse can prune monthly partitions. Critical: this is
the largest scan in the family (8B traces, 5+ years) — every monthly
batch must fit under the CH Cloud memory budget.
int_execution_safes.name: int_execution_safes

became_owner_at.description: Timestamp of the event that added (or set up) this owner. For an owner who was removed and re-added, this is the re-add time, not the original setup time.

current_threshold.name: current_threshold

owner.name: owner

int_execution_safes_current_owners.description:
Current owner set per Safe.

For each (safe_address, owner) pair, take the latest event by
(block_number, log_index). If the latest event is safe_setup or
added_owner, the owner is current; if removed_owner, the row is
dropped. Threshold is the latest safe_setup / changed_threshold per
Safe, denormalized onto every owner row.

`became_owner_at` is the timestamp of the most recent event that
added (or set up) this owner — for an owner who was removed and then
re-added, it is the re-add time, not the original setup time.

Cheap to fully rebuild every run; no incremental strategy needed.
int_execution_safes_current_owners.name: int_execution_safes_current_owners

int_execution_safes_module_events.description:
Long-form log of module-state and guard-state mutations on every Safe
in contracts_safe_registry.

event_kind ∈ (enabled_module, disabled_module, changed_guard,
changed_module_guard).

target_address is whichever address the event carried:
  • enabled_module / disabled_module → decoded_params['module']
  • changed_guard                    → decoded_params['guard']
  • changed_module_guard             → decoded_params['moduleGuard']

decode_logs owns the time-window filter (start_month/end_month + the
this-table incremental high-water mark). The outer model only mirrors
the strategy switch.
int_execution_safes_module_events.name: int_execution_safes_module_events

int_execution_safes_owner_events.description:
Long-form ownership-event log for every Safe in contracts_safe_registry.

All ABI decoding is delegated to decode_logs(...) so no byte-offset
arithmetic lives in this repo. The wrapping model only:
  • unrolls SafeSetup.owners[] into one row per initial owner
  • normalizes AddedOwner / RemovedOwner / ChangedThreshold into the
    same (event_kind, owner, threshold) shape

IMPORTANT — incremental filtering:
  decode_logs handles its own var('start_month') / var('end_month')
  window AND its own `` max-timestamp incremental predicate.
  This model must NOT add another WHERE on block_timestamp — doing so
  would shadow decode_logs's filter and break incremental runs. The
  only thing the outer model mirrors from the batch pattern is the
  config-level incremental_strategy switch.

Decoded array shape:
  decode_logs emits arrays as toJSONString([...]) under the param name,
  so decoded_params['owners'] is a JSON array string like
  '["0xabc","0xdef"]'. ClickHouse JSONExtract* functions are 1-indexed.
int_execution_safes_owner_events.name: int_execution_safes_owner_events

int_execution_zodiac_module_proxies.description:
Catalogue of every Zodiac module proxy ever deployed on Gnosis Chain.

Decoded directly from the canonical Zodiac ModuleProxyFactory's
ModuleProxyCreation(address indexed proxy, address indexed masterCopy)
event. The factory address is hardcoded — there has only ever been one
on Gnosis Chain at the canonical address.

decode_logs handles its own start_month/end_month + incremental filter,
so this model just sets the matching incremental_strategy switch and
reshapes the decoded output. Do NOT add an outer block_timestamp filter.

Foundation for downstream module-aware models — joined to
int_execution_safes_module_events to identify which Zodiac module
proxies are attached to which Safes (and to filter to a specific module
type via the master_copy column).
int_execution_zodiac_module_proxies.name: int_execution_zodiac_module_proxies

amount_usd.description: Conservative USD notional. When both sides priced: least(bought_usd, sold_usd). When only one side priced: that side's USD. When neither priced: NULL.
amount_usd.name: amount_usd

token_bought_price_usd.description: USD price for the bought token (ASOF join on daily prices, last 7 days).
token_bought_price_usd.name: token_bought_price_usd

int_live__dex_trades_raw.description:
Cached, rolling 48h window of unified DEX swaps across all supported protocols (Uniswap V3, Swapr V3, Balancer V2, Balancer V3) on Gnosis Chain. Normalizes amounts by token decimals, resolves symbols, and attaches USD prices per side via ASOF join against daily token prices. The final `amount_usd` uses the LEAST of the two priced sides when both are known (a conservative notional that rejects inflated long-tail prices), or the single available price when only one side is priced.
MATERIALIZATION: incremental ReplacingMergeTree, refreshed by a k8s CronJob running `dbt run --select +tag:live` every ~5 min. First run backfills the last 48h (source TTL). Each subsequent run delete+inserts the last `live_trades_overlap_minutes` (default 120 = 2h) to self-heal against cryo-live's out-of-order / bulk-attach inserts. 48h TTL on `block_timestamp` prevents unbounded growth and matches the source. Downstream api views read this table directly, so dashboard queries never trigger decode-log scans over `execution_live.logs`.
int_live__dex_trades_raw.name: int_live__dex_trades_raw

PoolId → pool_address is resolved via the materialized `stg_pools__balancer_v2_pool_registry` table (built from historical PoolRegistered events). Using a pre-materialized registry keeps this view cheap to query from the dashboard; re-scanning the full historical Vault events on every poll would be prohibitive.

stg_live__dex_trades_swapr_v3.description: Normalized Swapr V3 (Algebra) Swap events from execution_live logs. Same schema/logic as Uniswap V3 — Algebra uses the same signed `amount0` / `amount1` convention.

token_sold_address.description: Address of the token the swapper paid in.
token_sold_address.name: token_sold_address

stg_live__dex_trades_uniswap_v3.description: Normalized Uniswap V3 Swap events from execution_live logs. Derives bought/sold token & amounts from the signed `amount0` / `amount1` deltas and the pool's token0/token1 registry.
stg_live__dex_trades_uniswap_v3.name: stg_live__dex_trades_uniswap_v3

pool_id.description: Balancer V2 poolId (bytes32 hex string, lowercase).
pool_id.name: pool_id

stg_pools__balancer_v2_pool_registry.description: Mapping of Balancer V2 poolId (bytes32) → pool contract address, built from PoolRegistered events on the Vault. Materialized as a small table so that downstream views — especially the live-trades staging model queried on every dashboard poll — can join against it cheaply instead of re-scanning the full historical Vault events table.
stg_pools__balancer_v2_pool_registry.name: stg_pools__balancer_v2_pool_registry

token_address.description: Wrapper token address held by the pool (lowercase, with 0x prefix). Resolve to underlying via stg_pools__balancer_v3_token_map for price lookups.
token_address.name: token_address

token_index.description: 0-based position of this token in the pool, matching the index of amountsAddedRaw/amountsRemovedRaw in liquidity events.
token_index.name: token_index

stg_pools__balancer_v3_pool_tokens.description: Ordered token list per Balancer V3 pool derived from Swap events. Balancer V3 stores tokens in ascending address order; ROW_NUMBER() over sorted unique token addresses reproduces the same 0-based index used in LiquidityAdded/LiquidityRemoved amountsRaw arrays. Tokens are the ERC4626 wrappers the pool actually holds (e.g. waGnoGNO, waGnoWETH) — resolve to underlying via stg_pools__balancer_v3_token_map for price lookups. Only pools with at least one Swap event appear; pools with deposited liquidity but zero swaps are excluded (negligible activity in practice).
stg_pools__balancer_v3_pool_tokens.name: stg_pools__balancer_v3_pool_tokens

underlying_address.description: Underlying token address that the wrapper resolves to.
underlying_address.name: underlying_address

stg_pools__balancer_v3_token_map.description: Balancer V3 ERC4626 wrapper → underlying token mapping. Used to resolve wrapped Aave tokens (waGno*) to their underlying for price lookups and token symbol resolution. Hardcoded for the 5 Aave wrappers on Gnosis Chain; wrapper tokens are ~1:1 with underlying (plus accrued yield).
stg_pools__balancer_v3_token_map.name: stg_pools__balancer_v3_token_map

event_type.description: Direction/type of event: mint (add liquidity), burn (remove liquidity), or collect (claim fees; V3 only).
event_type.name: event_type

liquidity_delta.description: Signed change in liquidity units. Positive for Mint, negative for Burn, NULL for Collect and Balancer events. Same value on both token rows of the same event.
liquidity_delta.name: liquidity_delta

tick_lower.description: Lower tick bound of the position. Set for V3 Mint/Burn/Collect events, NULL for Balancer.
tick_lower.name: tick_lower

tick_upper.description: Upper tick bound of the position. Set for V3 Mint/Burn/Collect events, NULL for Balancer.
tick_upper.name: tick_upper

stg_pools__dex_liquidity_balancer_v2.description: Balancer V2 PoolBalanceChanged events with JSON array unpacking for multi-token pools. tick_lower, tick_upper, and liquidity_delta are NULL (full-range pools, no concentrated liquidity). Ephemeral.
stg_pools__dex_liquidity_balancer_v2.name: stg_pools__dex_liquidity_balancer_v2

event_type.description: Direction/type of event: mint (add liquidity), burn (remove liquidity), or collect (claim fees; V3 only).
event_type.name: event_type

liquidity_delta.description: Signed change in liquidity units. Positive for Mint, negative for Burn, NULL for Collect and Balancer events. Same value on both token rows of the same event.
liquidity_delta.name: liquidity_delta

tick_lower.description: Lower tick bound of the position. Set for V3 Mint/Burn/Collect events, NULL for Balancer.
tick_lower.name: tick_lower

tick_upper.description: Upper tick bound of the position. Set for V3 Mint/Burn/Collect events, NULL for Balancer.
tick_upper.name: tick_upper

stg_pools__dex_liquidity_balancer_v3.description: Balancer V3 LiquidityAdded/LiquidityRemoved events with positional array unpacking and wrapper-to-underlying token resolution. tick_lower, tick_upper, and liquidity_delta are NULL (full-range pools). Ephemeral.
stg_pools__dex_liquidity_balancer_v3.name: stg_pools__dex_liquidity_balancer_v3

event_type.description: Direction/type of event: mint (add liquidity), burn (remove liquidity), or collect (claim fees; V3 only).
event_type.name: event_type

liquidity_delta.description: Signed change in liquidity units. Positive for Mint, negative for Burn, NULL for Collect and Balancer events. Same value on both token rows of the same event.
liquidity_delta.name: liquidity_delta

tick_lower.description: Lower tick bound of the position. Set for V3 Mint/Burn/Collect events, NULL for Balancer.
tick_lower.name: tick_lower

tick_upper.description: Upper tick bound of the position. Set for V3 Mint/Burn/Collect events, NULL for Balancer.
tick_upper.name: tick_upper

stg_pools__dex_liquidity_swapr_v3.description: Swapr V3 (Algebra) Mint, Burn, and Collect events. Same structure as Uniswap V3. Uses bottomTick/topTick (Algebra naming) mapped to tick_lower/tick_upper. Ephemeral.
stg_pools__dex_liquidity_swapr_v3.name: stg_pools__dex_liquidity_swapr_v3

event_type.description: Direction/type of event: mint (add liquidity), burn (remove liquidity), or collect (claim fees; V3 only).
event_type.name: event_type

liquidity_delta.description: Signed change in liquidity units. Positive for Mint, negative for Burn, NULL for Collect and Balancer events. Same value on both token rows of the same event.
liquidity_delta.name: liquidity_delta

tick_lower.description: Lower tick bound of the position. Set for V3 Mint/Burn/Collect events, NULL for Balancer.
tick_lower.name: tick_lower

tick_upper.description: Upper tick bound of the position. Set for V3 Mint/Burn/Collect events, NULL for Balancer.
tick_upper.name: tick_upper

stg_pools__dex_liquidity_uniswap_v3.description: Uniswap V3 Mint, Burn, and Collect (fee claim) events unpacked into one row per token per event. Includes tick range and liquidity delta for position tracking. Ephemeral — inlined into int_execution_pools_dex_liquidity_events.
stg_pools__dex_liquidity_uniswap_v3.name: stg_pools__dex_liquidity_uniswap_v3

stg_pools__dex_trades_balancer_v2.description: Balancer V2 Vault Swap events with tokenIn/tokenOut fields. Pool ID resolved to pool address via PoolRegistered. Ephemeral.
stg_pools__dex_trades_balancer_v2.name: stg_pools__dex_trades_balancer_v2

stg_pools__dex_trades_balancer_v3.description: Balancer V3 Vault Swap events with wrapper-to-underlying token resolution via stg_pools__balancer_v3_token_map. Ephemeral.
stg_pools__dex_trades_balancer_v3.name: stg_pools__dex_trades_balancer_v3

stg_pools__dex_trades_swapr_v3.description: Swapr V3 (Algebra fork) swap events. Same sign logic as Uniswap V3. Ephemeral.
stg_pools__dex_trades_swapr_v3.name: stg_pools__dex_trades_swapr_v3

stg_pools__dex_trades_uniswap_v3.description: Uniswap V3 swap events decoded from Pool Swap events. Determines bought/sold via amount0/amount1 sign convention. Ephemeral — inlined into int_execution_pools_dex_trades_raw.
stg_pools__dex_trades_uniswap_v3.name: stg_pools__dex_trades_uniswap_v3

stg_pools__swapr_v3_events.description: Normalized event deltas for Swapr V3 (Algebra) pools. Same structure as stg_pools__uniswap_v3_events.
stg_pools__swapr_v3_events.name: stg_pools__swapr_v3_events

token.description: Uppercase token symbol (e.g. WETH, USDC.E). Null for empty/whitespace-only symbols.
token.name: token

token_address.description: Lowercase token contract address.
token_address.name: token_address

stg_pools__tokens_meta.description: Normalized token metadata from the tokens_whitelist seed. Provides lowercase address, uppercase symbol (nullable via nullIf), decimals, and validity window (date_start/date_end). Single source of truth for token_address → symbol mapping across all pool models.
stg_pools__tokens_meta.name: stg_pools__tokens_meta

delta_amount_raw.description: Signed raw token delta (positive = tokens entering pool, negative = leaving).
delta_amount_raw.name: delta_amount_raw

delta_category.description: Classification for fee separation logic. Values: liquidity (Mint/Burn), swap_in (positive swap side), swap_out (negative swap side), fee_collection (Collect), flash_fee (Flash).
delta_category.name: delta_category

event_type.description: Event name (Mint, Burn, Swap, Collect, Flash).
event_type.name: event_type

token_position.description: Which token in the pair (token0 or token1).
token_position.name: token_position

stg_pools__uniswap_v3_events.description: Normalized event deltas for Uniswap V3 pools. Parses Mint, Burn, Swap, Collect, Flash events into signed token deltas per token position.
stg_pools__uniswap_v3_events.name: stg_pools__uniswap_v3_events

current_tick.description: Tick value from the most recent Swap event on this pool.
current_tick.name: current_tick

stg_pools__v3_current_tick.description: Latest tick per V3 pool derived from the most recent Swap event. Used to determine whether concentrated-liquidity LP positions are in-range. Covers Uniswap V3 and Swapr V3 pools; Balancer pools have no tick concept and are excluded.
stg_pools__v3_current_tick.name: stg_pools__v3_current_tick

created_at.description: Block timestamp of the factory creation event.
created_at.name: created_at

fee_tier_ppm.description: Static fee tier in parts per million. Set for UniV3 (100/500/3000/10000), NULL for Swapr (dynamic fees).
fee_tier_ppm.name: fee_tier_ppm

init_sqrt_price_x96.description: sqrtPriceX96 at pool initialization. String to preserve full precision.
init_sqrt_price_x96.name: init_sqrt_price_x96

init_tick.description: Tick at pool initialization (from Initialize event). NULL if pool was never initialized.
init_tick.name: init_tick

pool_address_no0x.description: Pool contract address without 0x prefix (for join compatibility).
pool_address_no0x.name: pool_address_no0x

tick_spacing.description: Tick spacing for the pool. For Swapr, reflects latest TickSpacing event if changed.
tick_spacing.name: tick_spacing

stg_pools__v3_pool_registry.description: Complete registry of Uniswap V3 and Swapr V3 (Algebra) pools on Gnosis Chain. Combines factory creation events with pool Initialize events to produce a single source of truth for pool metadata. Includes fee tier (static for UniV3, NULL for Swapr with dynamic fees), tick spacing, initialization price/tick, and creation timestamp. Replaces the former int_execution_yields_v3_pool_meta view.
stg_pools__v3_pool_registry.name: stg_pools__v3_pool_registry

overrides.summary: Sector-level daily transaction count metric used as a semantic branch root for cross-metric joins.
